Article Title: LTM launches AI 1000 to train 1,000-plus AI engineers
Article Text:
Representative Image

Four-stage talent model







LTM Launches AI 1000 to Build Next Generation of Forward Deployed Engineers LTM has announced the launch of AI 1000, a major workforce transformation initiative anchored by a new Center of Excellence (CoE) in Bengaluru. The program aims to develop a pool of more than 1,000 AI-certified engineers, including Forward Deployed Engineers (FDEs), to help enterprises adopt, deploy, and scale AI solutions.The FDE role is among the fastest-growing in the AI ecosystem, combining technical expertise in large language models (LLMs) and domain-specific models (SLMs) with business acumen to deliver measurable ROI. LTM’s AI 1000 program is designed to build this talent at scale, providing structured training, platforms, and career frameworks to accelerate enterprise AI adoption.AI 1000 follows a four-stage model: Identify, Enable, Deploy, and Govern.* Identification begins with a proprietary AI Readiness Index to spot high-potential engineers.* Curated learning journeys focus on AI-native skills, validated through hackathons and real-world use cases.* Qualified engineers are deployed into AI programs.* A governance framework tracks performance, captures insights, and feeds learnings back into the system, creating a continuous cycle of capability building.Venu Lambu, CEO and Managing Director of LTM, said: “The role of the technology engineer is evolving rapidly. AI 1000 is built with the purpose of enhancing workforce productivity in creating tangible business outcomes. Through the AI 1000 CoE, we are building structured pathways to develop the combination of technical excellence and domain expertise to enable this purpose — and prepare our talent for the future. ”LTM has already logged 6.5 million learning hours, achieved 84% learning penetration, and secured 15,000+ external AI certifications alongside 24,000 AI-trained associates. AI 1000 formalizes these efforts into a structured, evidence-based program with defined role pathways, measurable milestones, and governed deployment frameworks. Success will be measured not by the number of employees trained, but by the business outcomes those engineers deliver.
Article Summary: LTM Launches AI 1000 to Build Next Generation of Forward Deployed Engineers. The program aims to develop a pool of more than 1,000 AI-certified engineers. LTM has already logged 6.5 million learning hours, achieved 84% learning penetration.

Article Title: Quote of the day by Jamie Dimon: You can compromise without violating your principles, but it is nearly impossible to compromise when...
Article Text:
AI generated image for representation

"You can compromise without violating your principles, but it is nearly impossible to compromise when you turn principles into ideology."

What Jamie Dimon's quote means



A lesson in leadership and collaboration



Why the quote matters still today







JPMorgan Chase chairman and CEO Jamie Dimon believes that effective decision-making requires both strong principles and a willingness to consider different perspectives. In one of his notable quotes, the veteran banker explained why compromise becomes difficult when people allow their beliefs to harden into ideology.The full quote reads:At its core, the quote highlights the difference between principles and ideology. Principles are the values that guide a person's actions and decisions. They provide a foundation for judgment and help people stay consistent in what they believe is right.Dimon argues that it is possible to compromise without abandoning those values. People can work together, negotiate, and find common ground while remaining true to their core beliefs.The challenge arises when principles become ideology. In that situation, beliefs can become rigid and non-negotiable, leaving little room for discussion or alternative viewpoints. As a result, compromise becomes much more difficult because any concession may be viewed as a betrayal of those beliefs.The quote reflects Dimon's broader approach to leadership and problem-solving. Throughout his career, he has emphasised the importance of analysis, debate, and bringing the right people into the decision-making process.In another piece of advice, Dimon said: "Most decisions are not binary, and there are usually better answers waiting to be found if you do the analysis and involve the right people."The ideas suggest that successful leaders should not see issues in absolute terms. Rather, they must be open to new information and different points of view while staying grounded in their core principles.Dimon’s message is not just for business. In workplace disputes, political disputes, or personal relationships, discussion and compromise are often the way to resolve differences.This quote is a reminder that strong values don’t mean you can’t cooperate . By separating principles from ideology, people can more readily find practical solutions, build consensus and make progress even when opinions differ.
Article Summary: JPMorgan Chase chairman and CEO Jamie Dimon believes that effective decision- making requires both strong principles and a willingness to consider different perspectives. In one of his notable quotes, the veteran banker explained why compromise becomes difficult when people allow their beliefs to harden into ideology.

Article Title: FIFA World Cup 2026: How tech inside official match ball Trionda makes it ‘special’
Article Text:
What is Trionda



Chip system in Trionda and charging



How this AI chip system helps the game



Faster offside call:

Touch recognition:

Aerodynamics and exterior design







The FIFA World Cup 2026 is underway across North America and the focus is not just on the players, but also on the equipment they are using, especially the official match ball. The tournament marked the debut of “Trionda” created by Adidas – the ball that represents a major shift in sports engineering, combining a symbolic design that honours the three host nations with a re-engineered internal tech system that directly assists referees.The name Trionda translates from Spanish as “three waves” – a reference to the joint hosting duties of Canada, Mexico and the US. Its appearance and physical structure are designed around this theme, but the defining feature of the ball is what sits underneath its outer layer. In previous tournament balls that featured tracking technology, the electronic sensor was suspended directly in the center of the ball using a system of tension chords.The Trionda changes this setup entirely as Adidas has introduced a side-mounted chip system. The tracking sensor now sits inside a specially manufactured layer within one of the ball's four main panels. Now those with football knowledge understand that this placement may make ball behave in an unusual pattern, but to ensure this off-center placement does not disrupt how the ball moves, counter-balances have been built into the other three panels.This design maintains the balance and flight stability of the ball when it is kicked, passed or crossed. Moreover, one of the most unusual things about Trionda is that since it has electronics inside, every Trionda ball must be charged before it is used in a match.The core of the ball’s technology is a 500Hz inertial measurement unit (IMU) motion sensor chip, developed in collaboration with tech firm Kinexon. The sensor measures the movement of the ball 500 times per second, capturing data on its speed, spin and direction.This data is transmitted in real time to the Video Assistant Referee (VAR) system. The technology helps match officials in two main ways:The ball data, combined with player-tracking cameras and processed by artificial intelligence (AI), identifies the exact millisecond the ball was kicked. This allows the VAR system to identify offside positions much more quickly than manual video reviews.”The sensor detects the exact moment the ball is touched. This makes it easier for referees to see who touched the ball individually, reducing time spent looking over contentious areas such as possible handballs or who last touched the ball before it left the line.The technology in the Trionda ball does not end with its internal chip system. The exterior of the ball consists of a new four-panel construction featuring fluid geometry. The panels connect to form a triangle at the center of the ball, symbolizing the union of the three host countries.The panels are thermally bonded, which reduces water absorption in wet weather, and improves aerodynamic flow. The four-panel layout has intentionally deep seams. These seams cause a certain amount of evenly distributed drag when the ball is flying through the air, which stabilizes the flight path and prevents unpredictable movement.The ball is covered with a polyurethane skin with a butyl bladder. It helps the ball retain air pressure and keep its shape over 90 minutes of play. The surface also has embossed icons. You can only see these icons when you look closely , but they give players additional grip when dribbling or kicking the ball in high humidity or rainy conditions .
Article Summary: FIFA World Cup 2026 is underway across North America. The tournament marked the debut of ‘Trionda’ created by Adidas. The ball combines a symbolic design that honours the three host nations with a re-engineered internal tech system that directly assists referees.

Article Title: MrBeast becomes first creator in YouTube history to hit 500 million subscribers: Inside his record-breaking rise to global fame
Article Text:
PC: YouTube

MrBeast became YouTube's first creator with 500 million subscribers



How MrBeast built a YouTube empire through spectacle and scale



MrBeast’s international reach behind 500 million YouTube subscribers



Philanthropy as part of the channel's identity







For years, YouTube's subscriber counts have served as a rough measure of influence, though the numbers themselves often tell only part of the story. Channels rise and fall, trends move quickly, and audiences rarely stay fixed for long. Yet every so often, a milestone appears that stands apart from the platform's usual cycle of records and rankings.As reported by the official YouTube blog, this week, Jimmy Donaldson, better known online as MrBeast, crossed the 500 million subscriber mark on YouTube. The figure places him in territory no individual creator has reached before. It is the latest chapter in a career that has transformed from a teenager experimenting with videos into one of the most recognisable names on the internet. The scale of the audience is difficult to picture. Half a billion subscribers represents a following larger than the populations of most countries, gathered around a channel that began with simple uploads and gradually evolved into some of the platform's most ambitious productions.The journey to this point was not marked by a single breakthrough moment. Instead, it unfolded over several years as MrBeast steadily expanded the scale of his content and the size of his audience.His channel gained momentum through increasingly elaborate challenge videos, large cash giveaways and projects that often resembled television productions in both scope and budget. As viewership grew, subscriber milestones arrived at an unusually rapid pace. By late 2022, he had become YouTube's most-subscribed individual creator. Less than two years later, he overtook every other channel on the platform. The latest figure pushes that lead even further and places him in a category of his own.Part of MrBeast's appeal has been his ability to make online videos feel like major events. Many creators build audiences around personalities or niche interests. His approach has often centred on spectacle.Over time, videos became larger, more expensive and more complicated to produce. Contest-based formats proved particularly successful, with participants competing for substantial prizes in carefully designed challenges. Some productions involved custom-built sets, large casts and months of planning before filming even began.Several projects blurred the line between traditional entertainment and digital content. Massive creator competitions featuring dozens of online personalities drew audiences from across different corners of YouTube, creating the sort of crossover appeal more commonly associated with mainstream broadcast events.Growth in subscribers on YouTube has taken an international route in recent years, and MrBeast’s development has taken into account the change. While the success was achieved using videos targeted at English-speaking audiences earlier on, there have been developments which ensure that the videos have gone to other regions of the world using translated versions of the content. Content that can be understood without necessarily knowing the language will reach more people. As such, the achievement does not just symbolize success in one particular market. It is success in multiple markets, and in several different languages too.While entertainment remains at the centre of the brand, charitable campaigns have become a recurring feature of MrBeast's public profile.One of the earliest large-scale efforts focused on tree planting. The initiative brought together creators, viewers and organisations around a fundraising target that eventually supported the planting of millions of trees. That project was followed by another environmental campaign aimed at removing waste from waterways and coastal areas. Both efforts attracted participation from communities well beyond MrBeast's own audience and demonstrated how creator-led campaigns could mobilise support on a global scale.Five hundred million subscribers is a number too large to feel real, which may be exactly why it matters less than what produced it. MrBeast did not build an audience by being famous. He built fame by making things people wanted to watch and then kept making them bigger until the audience ran out of reasons to leave.
Article Summary: Jimmy Donaldson, better known online as MrBeast, crossed the 500 million subscriber mark on YouTube. The figure places him in territory no individual creator has reached before. It is the latest chapter in a career that has transformed from a teenager experimenting with videos into one of the most recognisable names on the internet.

Article Title: Iran says 4 banks hit by hacking, services disrupted: Report
Article Text:
Representative Image

US-Iran ‘digital war’







A cyberattack has disrupted operations at four major Iranian banks, local media reported , adding that sensitive customer data remains safe. Citing state media reports, news agency Reuters said that Iran’s banking coordination council announced on Sunday (June 14) that a hacking incident successfully targeted a shared communications system used by the financial institutions.The disruption temporarily knocked out several banking services for customers across the country. The hit list included some of Iran's most prominent financial institutions, including Bank Melli, Bank Tejarat, Bank Saderat and The Export Development Bank of Iran.According to a post by London-based publication Iran International English, the technical team detected unusual signs and took necessary measures to isolate the attack.A limited cyberattack on shared communications infrastructure caused disruptions at four Iranian banks, the coordination council of banks said on Sunday.The council said the disruption affected Bank Melli Iran, Bank Tejarat, Bank Saderat Iran and the Export Development Bank of Iran.It said technical teams took protective measures after detecting unusual signs, adding that there had been no unauthorized access to customer data and no deletion of information.Earlier this year, the Trump administration issued a warning, saying that hackers backed by Iran’s Islamic Revolutionary Guards Corps had begun a series of cyberattacks on water and energy systems across the US. It was issued by the Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA), which did not name specific American facilities that had been struck or say whether any damage had been done.According to a report by The New York Times, CISA said that the attacks were focusing on equipment made by a major American producer of computer controllers, in an effort to make government agencies and utilities lock down their systems before the Iranian hackers can disrupt more systems.
Article Summary: The disruption temporarily knocked out several banking services for customers across the country. The hit list included some of Iran's most prominent financial institutions, including Bank Melli, Bank Tejarat, Bank Saderat and The Export Development Bank of Iran. The technical team detected unusual signs and took necessary measures to isolate the attack.
